agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A production logistics system enforces a strict 1:1 Shipment-to-Carrier invariant with supervisor-gated reassignment. The product team has requested an 'auto-split' feature that would automatically divide heavy shipments (>500 kg) across multiple carriers without supervisor involvement, directly conflicting with two established domain rules.","bounded_context":"Shipment Fulfillment / Carrier Assignment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A unit of freight to be delivered, which must have exactly one assigned Carrier at all times from assignment through dispatch."},{"term":"Carrier","definition":"The transport provider assigned to fulfill a Shipment."},{"term":"Carrier Assignment","definition":"The binding of exactly one Carrier to a Shipment. Once made, this assignment is locked and only a Supervisor can change it."},{"term":"Supervisor","definition":"A privileged role that is the sole authority permitted to reassign a Carrier on a Shipment."},{"term":"Sub-Shipment","definition":"A proposed new concept: a child Shipment created by splitting a parent Shipment that exceeds a weight threshold. Not yet part of the domain model."},{"term":"Auto-Split","definition":"Proposed automatic decomposition of an overweight Shipment into multiple sub-shipments, each assigned to a different Carrier, bypassing Supervisor approval."},{"term":"Dispatch","definition":"The point at which a Shipment is released for delivery; the domain guarantees every Shipment has exactly one Carrier at this moment."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a core state-integrity constraint enforced system-wide. If violated, dispatch, tracking, billing, and liability attribution all break — the system cannot function with zero or multiple carriers on a single shipment.","testable_assertion":"For any Shipment in any state from 'assigned' through 'delivered', COUNT(assigned_carriers) == 1."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Once a Carrier is assigned to a Shipment, only a Supervisor can reassign it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is an access-control invariant protecting assignment integrity. Without it, automated or unauthorized processes could silently reroute freight, creating liability and audit gaps.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to change a Shipment's Carrier by a non-Supervisor actor must be rejected with an authorization error."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"At the time of dispatch, every Shipment must have exactly one Carrier (dispatch pre-condition).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint — a shipment leaves on exactly one truck with one carrier. Violating this makes the shipment undeliverable or creates split-liability with no tracking model.","testable_assertion":"The dispatch operation must fail if the Shipment does not have exactly one assigned Carrier."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The proposed auto-split feature would create sub-shipments each assigned to different carriers, meaning the original shipment's freight is handled by multiple carriers without supervisor involvement.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a proposed business policy, not yet an invariant. It represents a feature request that conflicts with existing invariants.","testable_assertion":"When a Shipment exceeds 500 kg, the system automatically creates N sub-shipments each with a distinct Carrier and no Supervisor approval step."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A Shipment exists with weight > 500 kg","when":"The auto-split feature is triggered","then":"The system must NOT assign multiple carriers to the single original Shipment, as this violates BR-1."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A Shipment is split into sub-shipments","when":"Each sub-shipment is created","then":"Each sub-shipment must independently satisfy BR-1 (exactly one Carrier assigned to each sub-shipment)."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"Sub-shipments are created via auto-split","when":"Carrier assignment occurs on each sub-shipment","then":"The assignment must either go through Supervisor approval (per BR-2) OR the domain model must be explicitly evolved to define auto-split as a new assignment pathway distinct from reassignment."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A parent Shipment has been auto-split into sub-shipments","when":"Any sub-shipment reaches dispatch","then":"That sub-shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier (BR-3 preserved)."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A parent Shipment is split","when":"The split completes","then":"The parent Shipment must transition to a terminal or composite state (e.g., 'split') and must NOT itself be dispatchable — only its children are."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Shipment weighs exactly 500 kg","expected_behavior":"No split occurs — the threshold is 'exceeds 500 kg', meaning strictly greater than. This boundary must be precisely defined."},{"scenario":"A sub-shipment itself exceeds 500 kg after splitting","expected_behavior":"Recursive splitting policy must be defined. Could lead to infinite loops or sub-shipments too small to be economical."},{"scenario":"Auto-split is triggered but no additional carriers are available","expected_behavior":"The system cannot leave sub-shipments without a carrier (BR-1). Must either queue, alert a Supervisor, or reject the split."},{"scenario":"Carrier assignment on one sub-shipment fails after others succeed","expected_behavior":"Partial split creates orphaned sub-shipments violating BR-1. Must be transactional — all-or-nothing — or have a rollback/compensation strategy."},{"scenario":"Supervisor attempts to reassign carrier on a sub-shipment","expected_behavior":"BR-2 should still apply to sub-shipments. Supervisor authority must extend to the new entity type."},{"scenario":"Customer or downstream system queries the original shipment after split","expected_behavior":"Tracking, billing, and SLA attribution for the parent shipment must be well-defined. Is the parent a virtual aggregate? Does the customer see sub-shipments or only the original?"},{"scenario":"Shipment weight is updated after carrier assignment but before dispatch, now exceeding 500 kg","expected_behavior":"Must define whether auto-split can occur post-assignment, which would require breaking an existing locked carrier assignment — directly triggering BR-2 conflict."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Splitting shipments across multiple carriers increases carrier coordination costs, may change rate structures (smaller shipments often cost more per kg), and complicates invoicing. Liability attribution for damaged goods becomes ambiguous across carriers.","operational":"Introduces a new entity (sub-shipment) and a parent-child relationship that every downstream system — tracking, dispatch, billing, reporting, SLA monitoring — must understand. This is not a small feature; it is a domain model evolution.","user":"Customers expect to track one shipment. Splitting it may confuse end-users unless the UX abstracts sub-shipments behind the parent. Supervisors lose oversight of carrier assignments for heavy shipments, reducing operational control.","risk":"HIGH. The feature as stated directly violates two production invariants (BR-1's spirit of one-carrier-per-freight-unit, and BR-2's supervisor-gated reassignment). Shipping it without resolving these contradictions risks data corruption, audit failures, and liability gaps.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Operations / Supervisors","impact":"Lose authority over carrier assignment for auto-split shipments. Their oversight role is bypassed, potentially creating unmonitored carrier selections."},{"group":"Carriers","impact":"May receive partial loads they didn't bid on. Contractual and SLA implications of handling sub-shipments vs full shipments."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Shipment tracking, delivery experience, and SLA guarantees may change. A single order now arrives in multiple deliveries."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Invoice reconciliation becomes complex. Must attribute costs across multiple carriers for what was originally one shipment."},{"group":"Engineering","impact":"Must evolve the core domain model, not just add a feature. Every system consuming Shipment events must handle the new parent-child topology."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The spec contains two direct contradictions between established production invariants and the proposed feature. The feature as literally described cannot be implemented without violating BR-1 (if applied to the original shipment) or BR-2 (bypassing supervisor for carrier assignments). The product team may intend for sub-shipments to be first-class Shipments that individually satisfy BR-1, but this is not stated and would still conflict with BR-2. Too many unresolved conflicts to score above 50.","high_confidence_areas":["The existing invariants BR-1, BR-2, BR-3 are clearly stated and in production","The feature request as written directly conflicts with these invariants","Sub-shipment as a concept does not yet exist in the domain model"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether product team intends sub-shipments to be independent Shipments or a new entity type","Whether the 500 kg threshold is inclusive or exclusive","Whether auto-split should happen at creation, at assignment, or at dispatch","How billing, tracking, and SLA attribution work across sub-shipments"],"assumptions":["The existing domain model and invariants are accurately described and currently enforced in production","The product team has not yet considered the invariant conflicts","Sub-shipments would need to be a new first-class domain concept"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"BR-1 states a Shipment must have exactly one Carrier. The auto-split feature requires a single shipment's freight to be handled by multiple carriers. If sub-shipments are introduced as new Shipment entities, each can satisfy BR-1 individually, but this requires a domain model evolution that the feature request does not acknowledge or specify.","affected_stakeholders":["Engineering","Operations","Product"],"options":["Introduce a Sub-Shipment entity where each sub-shipment independently satisfies BR-1, and define the parent Shipment as a non-dispatchable aggregate","Reject the feature as incompatible with the current domain model","Redefine BR-1 to allow composite shipments with multiple carriers"],"recommendation":"Introduce Sub-Shipment as a first-class domain concept. Each sub-shipment satisfies BR-1 independently. The parent Shipment transitions to a 'split' state and is no longer directly dispatchable. This preserves the invariant at the sub-shipment level while enabling the business capability."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"BR-2 requires Supervisor involvement for any carrier reassignment. The auto-split feature explicitly requests bypassing Supervisor involvement for carrier assignment on new sub-shipments. These two rules cannot both hold — either auto-split requires Supervisor approval (defeating the 'automatic' intent) or BR-2 must be scoped to exclude initial assignment of newly created sub-shipments.","affected_stakeholders":["Operations / Supervisors","Product","Compliance"],"options":["Require Supervisor approval for auto-split (preserves BR-2 but undermines the feature's value)","Redefine BR-2 to distinguish between 'reassignment' (supervisor-gated) and 'initial assignment of a new sub-shipment' (system-automated)","Add a new role or policy that grants auto-split authority to the system under specific conditions, with Supervisor notification rather than approval"],"recommendation":"Distinguish between reassignment (changing an existing carrier binding, still supervisor-gated per BR-2) and initial assignment of a newly created sub-shipment (permissible by system automation). Auto-split creates new entities and assigns carriers — it does not reassign an existing binding. This semantic distinction preserves BR-2 while enabling the feature, but must be explicitly modeled and agreed upon by stakeholders."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"The auto-split feature as described assigns multiple carriers to one shipment's freight without supervisor involvement.","business_rule_broken":"BR-1: A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier. The feature implies multiple carriers handling one shipment."},{"violation":"Automatic carrier assignment on split shipments bypasses the supervisor-gated reassignment rule.","business_rule_broken":"BR-2: Only a Supervisor can reassign a Carrier on a Shipment. Auto-split assigns carriers to sub-shipments without any supervisor involvement."}]}
